title: A Farewell To Arms

jokes.text: Oh, my hair!
jokes.setup: Character reacts to unseen weather event
jokes.punchline: Single exclamation about hair priorities during crisis
jokes.explanation: Basic reaction comedy, economical but predictable

jokes.text: Lot of weather we're having. Allow me, m'lady.
jokes.punchline: Understated observation coupled with chivalrous gesture
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific understatement during chaos

jokes.text: Shall we go a-trousering?
jokes.punchline: Made-up archaic verb for pants retrieval
jokes.explanation: Clever neologism that fits Fry's overwrought romantic character perfectly

jokes.text: You're right. I'm sorry for showing my love.
jokes.punchline: Dramatically martyred response to mild criticism
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Fry's tendency to overreact romantically

jokes.text: My pants! My lucky pants!
jokes.punchline: Disproportionate emotional attachment to clothing
jokes.explanation: Sets up running gag about pants, good emotional investment

jokes.text: They don't look so lucky to me.
jokes.punchline: Dry observation about their current misfortune
jokes.explanation: Good straight-woman response, economical delivery

jokes.text: I was wearing them that time I found a dime in my ear. I was wearing them when I won a subscription to Redbook. And I was wearing them when I first met Leela
jokes.punchline: Increasingly pathetic examples culminating in meeting Leela
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from mundane to meaningless to romantic - very Fry

jokes.text: You've worn the same pants for a thousand years? No wonder they made a run for it.
jokes.setup: Learning Fry's only had one pair of pants
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizing pants as escaping, pun on 'run'
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay combining visual and verbal puns

jokes.text: I'm gonna blast that balloon! No way will I let God get my pants!
jokes.setup: Pants ascending to heaven in weather balloon
jokes.punchline: Declaring war on God over clothing
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd escalation - Fry vs. the Almighty over pants

jokes.text: Nobody messes with my pants! Not even the holy one, blessed be he.
jokes.punchline: Maintains reverence while threatening deity
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up maintaining religious respect while being combative

jokes.text: Look out! A Central Park badger!
jokes.punchline: Random dangerous wildlife in unexpected location
jokes.explanation: Random absurdist element, not particularly earned

jokes.text: I'll save you, pants!
jokes.punchline: Priority is still pants over people's safety
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - pants over personal safety

jokes.text: What viscosity you need?
jokes.punchline: Casual technical specificity about weird product
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy - treats bizarre request as routine maintenance

jokes.text: Also, this grease is flammable.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions critical safety information after the fact
jokes.explanation: Classic delayed warning gag, very Scruffy timing

jokes.text: No, this isn't Mutant language. We use a lot more profanity. Son of a bitch...!
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates with casual profanity
jokes.explanation: Good structure - claim followed by immediate demonstration

jokes.text: The one time your hands aren't sticky, they're greasy!
jokes.punchline: Murphy's law applied to Fry's hygiene habits
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about Fry's usual grossness

jokes.text: Oh, sorry. Normally I would've wiped them on my pants, but...
jokes.punchline: References lost pants situation
jokes.explanation: Nice callback connecting current problem to earlier setup

jokes.text: We made a rope from my shirt and jacket and an expedition flag from my underpants.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he got down to help
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate use of clothing items
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of makeshift equipment from clothing

jokes.text: Stop telling me to take your hand. Look, Fry, your noble gestures keep making things worse. Can't you just be a rude, unhelpful jerk like Bender?
jokes.setup: Pattern of Fry's failed helpful gestures
jokes.punchline: Suggests he emulate Bender's selfishness instead
jokes.explanation: Great character dynamics - loving criticism with Bender comparison

jokes.text: When I use up the toilet paper, I don't put on a new roll.
jokes.punchline: Proudly admits to mundane inconsideration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - petty selfishness delivered with pride

jokes.text: All right! Lucky pants!
jokes.punchline: Immediately validates his luck theory
jokes.explanation: Good callback reinforcing Fry's superstitious character

jokes.text: What badger could've built this?
jokes.punchline: Attributes impossible engineering to random animal
jokes.explanation: Weak callback - random connection to earlier badger

jokes.text: Ancient history coming through!
jokes.punchline: Someone crashes through destroying it
jokes.explanation: Standard slapstick - predictable destruction of precious artifact

jokes.text: predating even the 'Girls of Sumeria.'
jokes.punchline: Reference point is presumably pornographic calendar
jokes.explanation: Good Professor-style academic reference with inappropriate content

jokes.text: No, wait. There's some dried-up old stew on the screen.
jokes.punchline: Just food on the screen obscuring view
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - academic authority undermined by mundane reality

jokes.text: Sorry. I was eatin' a can of breakfast and lookin' at porn.
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to multitasking inappropriate activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy - inappropriate oversharing delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: I think it means... 'The sun will erupt... all shall perish,' blah-blah-blah. Get to the point! What does it say about me, Bender?
jokes.punchline: Bender only cares about personal relevance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender narcissism - apocalypse is only relevant if it affects him

jokes.text: The world? That's where I live! Told you it'd say something about me.
jokes.punchline: Self-centered logic connecting global catastrophe to himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bender logic - makes global disaster about his prediction being right

jokes.text: Because that's the year that's this year! See?
jokes.punchline: Professor explains obvious fact as revelation
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment - treating obvious information as scientific insight

jokes.text: Is it just me, or is the world ending more often these days?
jokes.punchline: Meta comment on frequency of world-ending episodes
jokes.explanation: Smart meta joke acknowledging show's apocalyptic episode frequency

jokes.text: Coward Man away!
jokes.punchline: Anti-heroic departure announcement
jokes.explanation: Good superhero parody - inverting heroic departure trope

jokes.text: Some of us were crazy before it was cool.
jokes.punchline: Claims hipster status for pre-existing mental issues
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg line - hipster logic applied to mental illness

jokes.text: It's not starting. Come ons, comes on!
jokes.punchline: Escape vehicle won't start at worst moment
jokes.explanation: Standard slapstick - car won't start trope in space context

jokes.text: Damn thing just won't turn over. It's like Pat on a Sunday morning.
jokes.punchline: Compares to wife's morning routine
jokes.explanation: Generic husband humor - not particularly clever or character-specific

jokes.text: Well, it wasn't a bad life. If only I could get back that time I spent watching TRON: Legacy.
jokes.punchline: Regrets watching specific bad movie
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about wasted time on bad entertainment

jokes.text: Before we die, I'm gonna find and destroy every remaining copy of TRON: Legacy. It may take a couple of hours, but...
jokes.punchline: Romantic gesture of destroying all copies worldwide
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry - grandiose romantic gesture that misses the point entirely

jokes.text: So... you wanna join the Balcony Club?
jokes.punchline: Awkward euphemism for sex
jokes.explanation: Good Fry awkwardness - inappropriate timing and phrasing

jokes.text: The Balcony Club? I have an individual membership.
jokes.punchline: Euphemism for masturbation
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay extending the club metaphor to solo activity

jokes.text: Zoidberg, get lost. - I am lost. So long.
jokes.punchline: Takes literally, admits actual lostness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg - literal interpretation plus pathetic self-awareness

jokes.text: Stop the end-of-the-world sex. We might survive after all.
jokes.punchline: Bluntly describes their activity
jokes.explanation: Good Amy bluntness - casually categorizes their desperate intimacy

jokes.text: All right, Amy, what's so important that you interrupted my embalming?
jokes.punchline: Was preparing own corpse
jokes.explanation: Good dark Professor humor - casually mentioning morbid preparation

jokes.text: That underground pyramid isn't a pyramid... it's a rocket ship! It was worth waiting five hours to hear you finish that sentence.
jokes.punchline: Bender sarcastically times her dramatic pause
jokes.explanation: Good Bender impatience with dramatic timing tropes

jokes.text: Hmm, I've never flown a pyramid before, but I used to drive around town in a mausoleum. How many people can this thing carry? Well, the mausoleum held ten horny teenagers, so... maybe 30,000.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre scaling from inappropriate past experience
jokes.explanation: Great absurd logic - inappropriate personal anecdote leads to massive overestimate

jokes.text: They had their chance. Let's go!
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandons altruism
jokes.explanation: Good Professor flip-flop from moral to selfish in record time

jokes.text: The obvious destination is Mars. It's close, with lots of open space and no Woodward or Bernstein.
jokes.punchline: Political paranoia about investigative journalists
jokes.explanation: Good Zapp paranoia - makes planetary choice about avoiding scrutiny

jokes.text: Who shall live and who shall die? Step right up to the contrabulous choose-matron!
jokes.punchline: Carnival barker presentation of life-or-death decision
jokes.explanation: Excellent neologism 'contrabulous' plus dark carnival atmosphere

jokes.text: Female, crotchety. Needed to keep others in their place. Accepted!
jokes.punchline: Values antisocial traits as useful
jokes.explanation: Good dark logic - negative personality trait becomes survival asset

jokes.text: We just got one of these. Rejected!
jokes.punchline: Casually rejects duplicate profession
jokes.explanation: Basic rejection humor - treating humans like commodities

jokes.text: Female, scientist. We were in the same sorority. Accepted! - Sigma Beta, see you latah! - Sigma Beta, see you latah!
jokes.punchline: Actually based on college connections
jokes.explanation: Good satire of nepotism disguised as merit-based selection

jokes.text: Male, medical doctor, delicious with butter and lemon. Accepted!
jokes.punchline: Includes culinary preparation as qualification
jokes.explanation: Dark humor suggesting Zoidberg's dual purpose as doctor and food

jokes.text: Mayhem?! There's gonna be mayhem? I'm stayin'! Hoo-ee!
jokes.punchline: Excitement about staying for violence
jokes.explanation: Good Scruffy moment - choosing chaos over safety

jokes.text: Delivery boy, no discernible skills. Accepted! 'Cause I like his pants.
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary acceptance based on clothing preference
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - pants luck finally works in completely arbitrary way

jokes.text: Top gun pilot, natural leader, extreme combat training. Rejected! With all spaceships inoperable, we only need one spaceship pilot.
jokes.punchline: Rejected for being too qualified for available position
jokes.explanation: Good irony - most qualified person rejected for rational but harsh reasons

jokes.text: Male, filthy hand, violent temper. Accepted again!
jokes.setup: Fry putting dirty hand in machine
jokes.punchline: Negative traits somehow qualify him twice
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to earlier hand grease, machine's bizarre logic

jokes.text: I wish I could remember with my boobs.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate anatomical reference
jokes.explanation: Cheap humor - anatomical joke without setup or character reason

jokes.text: A magician never reveals his secrets. Except the Great Revealo. That guy stinks.
jokes.punchline: Immediate exception and professional criticism
jokes.explanation: Good structure - sets up trope then immediately breaks it with specific example

jokes.text: Wait, Fry? I'm a lot of people, but I'm not Fry.
jokes.punchline: Bender's identity crisis while denying being Fry
jokes.explanation: Good Bender line - acknowledges his multiple personalities while making basic distinction

jokes.text: Admission, $20. - Dad! Gleesh.
jokes.punchline: Immediately charges admission fee
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leo Wong - monetizes humanitarian crisis immediately

jokes.text: Bender! You're stealing your own stuff! I am? Geez, I better slow down. I'm stealing stuff I don't even need. - You want a Torah? - Nah, I'm not hungry.
jokes.punchline: Stealing own possessions, treating religious text as food
jokes.explanation: Great Bender logic - confused about ownership and treats Torah as consumable

jokes.text: No. 'Cause as long as Leela lives, I'll be alive, too, in her heart. But really I'll be dead.
jokes.setup: Romantic sentiment about living on in memory
jokes.punchline: Immediately clarifies literal death
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry - tries romantic sentiment then immediately undercuts himself with reality

jokes.text: I give you... Dickfrancisco!
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau combining crude word with San Francisco
jokes.explanation: Juvenile wordplay - crude but economical city naming

jokes.text: The doves didn't magically spring from my hand, but rather were crammed into this netting sewn into my sleeve!
jokes.punchline: Immediately explains methodology, ruining magic
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to earlier joke - magician who always reveals secrets

jokes.text: And now, as a solemn testicle to those left behind on Earth
jokes.punchline: Malapropism substituting body part for 'testament'
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism - inappropriate word substitution during serious moment

jokes.text: That frightened little girl in the statue reminds me of Fry.
jokes.punchline: Compares heroic monument to Fry as scared child
jokes.explanation: Good character observation - deflates heroic monument with accurate Fry assessment

jokes.text: Who is this non-Washington redskin?
jokes.punchline: Nixon's confused sports reference mixing football teams with racial terminology
jokes.explanation: Nixon confusion playing on NFL team names. Character-appropriate but somewhat dated reference.

jokes.text: Not your world. Our world. We put calendar there to warn you not to visit Mars. It Mars that gonna be destroyed.
jokes.punchline: Revelation of translation error - Mars was the target
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot reversal - major story twist based on translation error

jokes.text: Why you think we so eager to abandon this dump?
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of home planet as worthless
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan - treats planetary abandonment as obvious real estate decision

jokes.text: Hey, Ancient Martian's a very hard language. A........ looks just like a .........
jokes.punchline: Visual demonstration of similar-looking symbols
jokes.explanation: Good Amy defense - shows why translation error was reasonable

jokes.text: Oh, what an idiot I was. And by 'I', I mean 'you.'
jokes.punchline: Immediately redirects blame to someone else
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - appears to take responsibility then immediately deflects blame

jokes.text: I should've known better than to trust one of Fry's romantic gestures. Every time he says he loves me, I get killed.
jokes.punchline: Pattern recognition of Fry's love causing her death
jokes.explanation: Great character awareness - recognizes absurd pattern in their relationship

jokes.text: Oh no! The Kajigger of Gibraltar!
jokes.punchline: Nonsense name for Rock of Gibraltar
jokes.explanation: Good neologism - 'kajigger' applied to famous landmark

jokes.text: I have got to quit drinking.
jokes.punchline: Attributes reality to alcohol hallucination
jokes.explanation: Good Bender reaction - blames impossible reality on his drinking

jokes.text: Wait! Help! I'm still on Mars, and I can't jump! Hang on, Leela! I'll save you again! Or somebody else could do it!
jokes.punchline: Immediately suggests delegation
jokes.explanation: Good Fry moment - heroic impulse immediately undercut by practical cowardice

jokes.text: No, we're not. Grab your severed arm with your other arm.
jokes.setup: Trying to rescue Leela from distance
jokes.punchline: Casual instruction about using dismembered limb as tool
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist problem-solving - treats severed arm as practical tool

jokes.text: Good thing Scruffy rescued Leela. Don't thank me, thank the ladder.
jokes.punchline: Scruffy deflects credit to inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy - modest to the point of crediting tools over himself

jokes.text: But for right now, enjoy the sounds of the world's smallest violin.
jokes.punchline: Plays tiny violin to mock their suffering
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic gesture - Professor's cruel bedside manner

jokes.text: I can't be mad. I'm on way too many painkillers.
jokes.setup: Asked if she's mad about arm injury
jokes.punchline: Drug-induced emotional numbness
jokes.explanation: Good drug humor - medical reality affecting emotional response

jokes.text: I mean, you failed, miserably, but you're the only person who loves me enough to try.
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty about his competence wrapped in romantic sentiment
jokes.explanation: Excellent Leela - perfect balance of harsh truth and romantic appreciation

tmdb_overview: An ancient prophecy predicts the world will end in the year 3012.
